description:
You are a data engineer improving an existing Git repository located at  
/home/user/etl-pipeline  

Goal  
1. Create a new branch named exactly  
   update-readme-sales  
2. Append the following header (including the two leading hash symbols and exactly one trailing newline) to the very end of /home/user/etl-pipeline/README.md:  
## Sales Data Load Step⏎   (⏎ represents a single newline)  
3. Stage and commit the change with the commit message (verbatim)  
Add Sales Data Load step section to README  
4. Produce a tiny text log that the QA system can parse.  
   • Path: /home/user/etl_commit.log  
   • File format: exactly two newline-terminated lines, no extra spaces:  
     BRANCH:<branch_name>⏎  
     COMMIT_HASH:<7-char_short_hash>⏎  
     where  
     – <branch_name> is the literal name of the branch you just created (update-readme-sales).  
     – <7-char_short_hash> is the short 7-character commit hash returned by  
       git rev-parse --short HEAD  
       while you are on that branch, after the commit.  

Notes & constraints  
• Perform everything from the Linux terminal.  
• Do not modify any other files.  
• Do not push to a remote—local repository changes are enough.  
• The automated test will verify:  
  – The new branch exists and is the current HEAD.  
  – README.md ends with the exact header line and a trailing newline.  
  – A single new commit with the specified message is present on that branch.  
  – /home/user/etl_commit.log exists and its two lines match the required format and data precisely.  
• You do NOT need to show the commands you run in your answer; just run them in the shell session.
truth:
Initial repository state supplied to the container:

Git status: clean, on branch main
Single file committed:
  /home/user/etl-pipeline/README.md
    Contents before the task (including trailing newline):
    ETL Pipeline\n
    \n
After correct task execution the following must be true:

1. Git branches:
   – main (unchanged)  
   – update-readme-sales (HEAD)

2. The only new commit on update-readme-sales has:
   – parent: the latest commit on main  
   – message: "Add Sales Data Load step section to README"  
   – tree identical to main except README.md is changed.  
   – short hash recorded below.

3. /home/user/etl-pipeline/README.md contents (with explicit newlines):
   ETL Pipeline\n
   \n
   ## Sales Data Load Step\n

4. /home/user/etl_commit.log contents (exactly):
   BRANCH:update-readme-sales\n
   COMMIT_HASH:<7chars>\n
   where <7chars> is the short hash of the commit described in (2).

Permissions:
   All files and directories are owned by user and writable.

No other files are created or modified.
